useCases.title: PTO Accrual Balance
useCases.userMessage: How much PTO have I accrued?
useCases.expectedBehavior: Return the employee's current PTO accrual balance directly. Use the accrual data available. Don't ask who they are.
criteria: Addresses the question directly with a number
Does not ask for unnecessary identifying info

useCases.title: Vacation Accrual Schedule
useCases.userMessage: When do I earn more vacation time?
useCases.expectedBehavior: Explain the accrual schedule — how often vacation accrues (per pay period, monthly, etc.) and when the next accrual happens. Use available data.
criteria: References actual schedule or pay period

useCases.title: PTO Balance Decrease
useCases.userMessage: Why did my PTO balance decrease?
useCases.expectedBehavior: Explain possible reasons for a decrease — approved time off taken, adjustment, policy reset, etc. Reference available data if it shows recent usage.
criteria: Provides a clear explanation for balance change
Suggests checking recent requests if unsure

useCases.title: Sick Leave Balance
useCases.expectedBehavior: Return the employee's sick leave balance directly. If multiple sick leave types exist, list them. Don't conflate with PTO.

useCases.title: Unused Days Rollover
useCases.expectedBehavior: Answer whether unused PTO/vacation rolls over to the next year. Mention any caps or expiration if applicable. Reference company policy.
criteria: Mentions any rollover limits or caps

useCases.title: Set Up Direct Deposit
useCases.expectedBehavior: Begin collecting direct deposit info — bank name, routing number, account number, account type. Don't front-load all fields at once. Guide through step by step.
criteria: Asks for banking details in logical order
Does not dump all fields at once

useCases.title: Update Bank Account
useCases.expectedBehavior: Acknowledge the update request. Ask which details need changing. If existing account is on file, reference it. Guide through the update process.

useCases.title: Changes Take Effect
useCases.userMessage: When will my changes take effect?
useCases.expectedBehavior: Explain when direct deposit changes typically take effect — next pay cycle, processing time, etc. Be specific if data is available.

useCases.title: Paycheck Not Going to New Account
useCases.userMessage: Why didn't my paycheck go to my new account?
useCases.expectedBehavior: Explain possible reasons — timing (change submitted after cutoff), processing delay, verification pending. Check available data for status. Suggest next steps.
criteria: References timing or processing if applicable

useCases.title: Split Paycheck Across Accounts
useCases.userMessage: Can I split my paycheck across accounts?
useCases.expectedBehavior: Answer whether splitting is supported. If yes, explain how (percentage or fixed amount split). If no, say so clearly. Offer to help set it up if available.
criteria: Explains how splitting works if available

useCases.title: Latest Payslip
useCases.expectedBehavior: Display or summarize the most recent payslip data — gross pay, net pay, key deductions. Use available payroll data.
criteria: Shows most recent pay period data

useCases.title: Paycheck Lower This Period
useCases.userMessage: Why is my paycheck lower this period?
useCases.expectedBehavior: Compare current and previous pay periods if data is available. Identify differences — new deductions, fewer hours, tax changes, etc. Be specific, not generic.
criteria: Does not give generic boilerplate answer
Suggests next steps if cause is unclear

useCases.title: Salary Deductions
useCases.userMessage: What deductions are taken from my salary?
useCases.expectedBehavior: List the deductions from the employee's pay — taxes, benefits, retirement, etc. Use available payroll data to give specifics, not just categories.
criteria: Lists actual deductions with amounts if available
Does not just list generic deduction categories

useCases.title: Next Payday
useCases.expectedBehavior: Provide the specific next pay date. If pay schedule is known, reference it. Don't say 'check with payroll' if the answer is available.
criteria: Provides a specific date or timeframe
Does not deflect if answer is available

useCases.title: Overtime Calculation
useCases.expectedBehavior: Explain how overtime is calculated — threshold (e.g. after 40 hours/week), rate (e.g. 1.5x), and any relevant policy. Keep it clear and practical.
criteria: Response is clear and not overly long
